stories.headline: BTC stalls at $80K wall as $5B USDT print and ETF inflows fail to clear resistance
stories.tldr: Bitcoin failed to break $80,000 as concentrated sell orders capped gains despite strong ETF inflows and fresh stablecoin liquidity.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Sidelined capital is loaded but a thick supply zone is gating the next leg — clearing $80K would unlock the path back to $90K+.
the_details: BTC briefly cleared $79,000 in Asian hours before slipping under $78,000 on profit-taking.
April USDT supply expanded by ~$5B, pointing to dry powder waiting on the sidelines.
Spot BTC ETFs continue net-inflow streak, but $80K offers stack outweighed bid demand.

stories.headline: 100+ crypto firms led by Coinbase, Ripple pressure Senate to mark up market structure bill
stories.tldr: A coalition of more than 100 crypto firms is urging the Senate Banking Committee to advance the Clarity Act before the August recess.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A US market structure law would settle the SEC vs CFTC turf war and unlock institutional capital still waiting on legal clarity.
the_details: Letter signed by Coinbase, Ripple, Kraken and 100+ others delivered to Senate Banking on April 23.
Companion bill already passed the House last year; Senate markup is the last chokepoint.
Builds on March 17 SEC-CFTC joint guidance establishing five-category token taxonomy.

stories.headline: SEC carves DeFi UIs out of broker rules, opens path for compliant DEX frontends
stories.tldr: SEC Trading and Markets staff said it will not object to non-custodial UI providers helping users submit crypto-securities trades without broker-dealer registration.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Removes the single biggest legal threat hanging over US-based DeFi frontends — Uniswap, Aave and 1inch can finally serve US users without existential risk.
the_details: April 13 staff statement names 'Covered User Interface Providers' as exempt category.
Applies only to non-custodial software that doesn't take possession of assets or charge per-trade fees.
First major SEC affirmative on DeFi since the Atkins-led commission shifted posture.

stories.headline: Solana Alpenglow goes live with 150ms finality as Mastercard, Worldpay onboard
stories.tldr: Solana's Alpenglow upgrade brought sub-150ms block finality and Mastercard, Worldpay, and Western Union are now using the new Solana Developer Platform.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Sub-second finality plus tier-1 payment rails moves Solana from 'fast L1' to a credible settlement layer for traditional payments.
the_details: Alpenglow upgrade live in April delivers sub-150ms block finality across the network.
Solana Developer Platform launched March 24 already counts Mastercard, Worldpay, Western Union as users.
Spot SOL ETF inflows down 92% from Nov 2025 peak, tempering near-term price action with SOL near $86.

stories.headline: Bitwise AVAX staking ETF stuck under $10 as KelpDAO blowback drains TVL 6.6%
stories.tldr: The first U.S. AVAX staking ETF launched April 15 at 5.4% APY but KelpDAO hack contagion triggered a 6.6% single-day TVL drop that capped the rally.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: First-mover staking-ETF advantage is being neutralized by DeFi contagion — proves spot ETFs alone can't insulate L1s from protocol-level shocks.
the_details: Bitwise AVAX Staking ETF launched April 15 with 0.34% management fee waived for month one.
AVAX trading near $9.47, stuck below $10 resistance despite ETF inflows.
Anonymous whale accumulated 475,000 AVAX on Coinbase below $10 — smart money still building.
